string-array.name: product_names
string-array.item: RJ45 Connector
Crimping Tool
Network Tester
Stripper
RJ45 Pass-through
Punch Down Tool
Lan Cutter
LAN Cable Cat6
Network Tool Kit
RJ45 Keystone Jack
Nylon Cable Ties

string-array.name: product_descriptions
string-array.item: The standard physical connector used for Ethernet networking. It features 8 pins that terminate the 8 wires within a standard twisted-pair network cable.

A specialized set of pliers used to "crimp" or fuse an RJ45 connector onto the end of a LAN cable. It pushes the metal contacts of the connector into the internal wires to create a solid electrical path.

A battery-operated device used to verify that a cable is wired correctly. It consists of a master unit and a remote unit; when connected to both ends of a cable, it lights up in sequence to show if there are any breaks or crossed wires.
A small tool with a protected blade designed to remove the outer plastic insulation (jacket) of a network cable without nicking the delicate copper wires inside.

An updated version of the standard RJ45 connector where the wires slide completely through the front of the plug. This allows you to verify the wire order before crimping, significantly reducing mistakes.

Used for inserting wires into "insulation-displacement connectors" (IDC) found on the back of patch panels or inside wall jacks (keystone jacks). It pushes the wire into the slot and trims the excess in one motion.
While often built into crimping tools, a dedicated LAN cutter is a sharp, flat-bladed tool used to get a perfectly flush, clean cut on a bundle of wires or a thick CAT6 cable.

Category 6 is a standardized twisted pair cable for Ethernet. Compared to Cat5e, it has stricter specifications for crosstalk and system noise and can support speeds up to 10 Gbps over shorter distances.

A comprehensive bundle—usually in a carrying case—that contains all the items mentioned above. It’s the "all-in-one" solution for technicians to carry their termination and testing gear to a job site.

Modular Ethernet connector that terminates network cables and provides clean, professional connections to wall plates or patch panels. Features easy punch-down installation and supports Cat5e/Cat6 standards for reliable data transmission.

Durable, self-locking plastic straps designed to bundle, secure, and organize cables, wires, and various objects with ease. Available in multiple sizes and colors to suit home, office, and industrial applications.
